nodes.label: AI as supplement to creative process
nodes.label_negative: AI as sole creator of formulaic content
nodes.grounding_span: I want them to be as exciting/humorous/eye catching as possible so sometimes the AI will provide me with a caption that gives me a new idea

nodes.label: AI as writing assistant
nodes.label_negative: AI as unnecessary for straightforward tasks
nodes.grounding_span: with the product descriptions, there isn't as much creativity required as I just need a description of a product that is grammatically sound and sounds professional

nodes.label: AI trustworthiness and verification
nodes.grounding_span: I'm not ready to trust an AI to help me with it just yet

nodes.label: Authenticity of AI-generated content
nodes.grounding_span: people are still wary that AI content can still be quite obvious and they don't want it to impact their viewership

nodes.label: Personal enjoyment tasks kept for oneself
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to do this myself as I enjoy taking photos and staging content so I wouldn't want to stop doing it

nodes.label: Creative authenticity and human expression
nodes.grounding_span: I want them to be as exciting/humorous/eye catching as possible

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: it saved me about 2 hours of work

nodes.label: Professional accuracy and reliability
nodes.grounding_span: I like to know that I have done it accurately and correctly

nodes.label: Personal fulfillment and skill mastery
nodes.grounding_span: I still enjoy it and I work with the AI to create social media captions which keeps my skills involved

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for time-saving
nodes.grounding_span: it was very satisfying as it provided which caption would suit each day of the week and varied them completely

nodes.label: Resistance to AI in creative work
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to do this myself as I enjoy taking photos and staging content so I wouldn't want to stop doing it

nodes.label: Openness to increasing AI use in future
nodes.grounding_span: I'm not ready to trust an AI to help me with it just yet. That isn't to mean I don't ever see myself using it for that stuff as there is a high chance I could

nodes.label: Awareness of authenticity concerns in social media field
nodes.grounding_span: people are still wary that AI content can still be quite obvious and they don't want it to impact their viewership

nodes.label: Task-dependent delegation vs. collaboration
nodes.grounding_span: With the caption generation, I come with a post in mind... With the product description, I state what the item is and let it write the whole description

nodes.label: Passion-driven task differentiation — distinguishes enjoyable from tedious tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to do this myself as I enjoy taking photos and staging content so I wouldn't want to stop doing it
